figures.caption: Figure 4. Evolution of denoising across time. Comparison of uniform discretization in time t (top) versus noise level σ (bottom). The logarithmic parameterization provides better coverage of the denoising trajectory with a focus on the early stages. All images not normalized.
Table 4. Binomial flow EDM (T = 1). Here µdata and σ2 data are the mean and variance of the unscaled images [0, 255], and εcin = 0.01 in our experiments.

Figure 7. Noise sampling distributions. The normalized improvement curve Lσ(b) − Lσ(m̂) (solid) compared to the induced distributions from uniform time sampling t ∼ U[0, 1] and matched Gaussian noise sampling.

Figure 1. The points in [0, T ]×[0, C] are generated according to d = 2 independent standard Poisson process (red and blue); 7 red points and 5 blue points. At time t ∈ [0, T ] the value of the process X1 t (res. X2 t ) is equal to the number of points under the curve λ1 (res. λ2) which are denoted as red (res. blue ) filled circles. In the figure X1 t=0.5 = 1 and X1 t=T = 4 (res. X2 t=0.5 = 2 and X2 t=T = 3).
Figure 8. Denoiser Poisson-Föllmer EDM samples for t = 0, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1.
Figure 6. True and estimated log-likelihood evaluations of the Poisson, ZeroInflated Poisson, and Yule-Simon distributions

Figure 3. Denoising at early time t = 0.001. Left column: noisy observations xt ∼ Binomialx1,t (normalized to fill the dynamic range). Middle column: denoiser predictions m(xt, t) ≈ E[X1|Xt = xt]. Right column: clean images x1 from CIFAR10. Two groups, six samples.
Table 3. Evaluation of the W1 metric (lower is better) between the target distribution and generated samples from Binomial Flows in comparison to itDPDM [BGB+25]
Table 2. CIFAR-10 unconditional generation results. FID (lower is better) is based on 50K generated samples. Methods are grouped by data representation: continuous (top) and discrete (bottom).
Figure 5. Unconditional CIFAR-10 samples generated using the Poisson-Föllmer process with EDM preconditioning.
Table 1. NLL evaluations for the target samples under the predicted and true distributions, showing close agreement.
Figure 2. Target dataset, generated samples using Binomial Flows and the true target PMF from four synthetic datasets in Section 3.1
